A Lean 4 library

STATLIB

Non-asymptotic mathematical statistics, from concentration inequalities to certified finite-sample learning guarantees.

GitHub repositoryLean-MoDS / Statlib↗

Abstract. Statlib develops concentration of measure, metric entropy and chaining, Rademacher complexity and uniform laws, random matrix theory, and sharp least-squares guarantees as formal mathematics. Definitions elaborate, proofs close, and the complete development is checked by the Lean 4 kernel.

666Theorems
1,219Lemmas
352Definitions
89Lean files
64,695Lines of Lean
0Sorries

Roman Vershynin

High-Dimensional Probability

High-Dimensional Probability: An Introduction with Applications in Data Science, 2nd ed. (Cambridge University Press, 2026).

Chapter 2

Concentration of sums of independent random variables

Exponential-moment and cumulant methods for Hoeffding’s lemma and Chernoff bounds, MGF-based sub-Gaussian scales and tails, independent sub-Gaussian linear combinations, finite maximal inequalities, Bernstein-type concentration from local CGF control, and a normalized bounded-density small-ball bound.

Statlib/Probability/Moments/{Cumulant, Exponential}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Concentration/{Hoeffding, Chernoff, Maximal, HansonWright}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Process/SubGaussian.lean · Statlib/Probability/SmallBall.lean
Chapter 3

Random vectors in high dimensions

Projection-based sub-Gaussian vector scales, isotropy through second moments, standard Gaussian and Rademacher models, spectral and Rayleigh-quotient infrastructure used in PCA, and quadratic-form concentration underlying Euclidean norm bounds.

Statlib/Probability/Process/SubGaussian.lean · Statlib/Probability/Gaussian/Basic.lean · Statlib/Probability/Concentration/HansonWright.lean · Statlib/Probability/RandomMatrix/Basic.lean · Statlib/LearningTheory/Rademacher/Signs.lean · Statlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/{SingularValue, CourantFischer}.lean
Section 4.1

Matrix foundations and perturbation

Courant–Fischer min–max principles, Weyl inequalities, Davis–Kahan perturbation bounds, and the Eckart–Young–Mirsky theorem.

Statlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/{SingularValue, CourantFischer, EckartYoungMirsky, Perturbation}.lean
Sections 4.4 & 4.6

Norms and singular values of random matrices

Operator-norm bounds for matrices with independent sub-Gaussian entries and two-sided singular-value bounds for isotropic sub-Gaussian rows.

Statlib/Probability/RandomMatrix/Basic.lean
Section 5.4

Matrix calculus, Lieb, and matrix Bernstein

Loewner-order matrix calculus, Lieb concavity, trace-MGF recursion, and the matrix Bernstein inequality.

Statlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/Lieb.lean · Statlib/Probability/RandomMatrix/{Lieb, Bernstein}.lean
Section 6.2

Hanson–Wright inequality

Concentration for quadratic forms of independent centered sub-Gaussian random variables.

Statlib/Probability/Concentration/HansonWright.lean
Chapter 8

Chaining and Dudley’s entropy integral

Covering numbers, metric entropy, dyadic chaining, separability reductions, and Dudley-type bounds for sub-Gaussian processes.

Statlib/Topology/MetricSpace/CoveringNumber/Basic.lean · Statlib/Analysis/MetricEntropy/{Basic, Chaining}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Process/{Dudley, TruncatedDudley}.lean · Statlib/Topology/SeparableSpace/Supremum.lean

Martin J. Wainwright

High-Dimensional Statistics

High-Dimensional Statistics: A Non-Asymptotic Viewpoint (Cambridge University Press, 2019).

Chapter 2

Concentration and sub-Gaussian variables

MGF control, ψ₂ norms, Hoeffding-type tails, Gaussian product measures, and Lipschitz concentration.

Statlib/Probability/Process/SubGaussian.lean · Statlib/Probability/Concentration/{Hoeffding, Chernoff}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Gaussian/{Basic, Lipschitz, LipschitzConcentration}.lean
Chapter 4

Uniform laws and symmetrization

Rademacher sign calculus, symmetrization, McDiarmid’s bounded-difference inequality, and uniform deviation bounds for countable and separable classes.

Statlib/LearningTheory/Rademacher/{Defs, Signs, Symmetrization, Complexity}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Concentration/McDiarmid.lean · Statlib/LearningTheory/UniformDeviation/{Defs, BoundedDifference, Bounds}.lean
Chapter 5

Metric entropy and empirical Dudley bounds

Entropy integrals, finite-class maximal inequalities, Massart’s lemma, and an empirical Dudley entropy bound.

Statlib/Analysis/MetricEntropy/Basic.lean · Statlib/Probability/Concentration/Maximal.lean · Statlib/Probability/Process/{FiniteMaximum, Dudley}.lean · Statlib/LearningTheory/Rademacher/{Massart, Dudley}.lean
Chapter 13

Localized complexity and least squares

Star-shaped classes, localized Gaussian complexity, the critical radius, master error bounds, the minimax rate for linear regression, and covering/localization infrastructure for ℓ¹-constrained regression.

Statlib/Statistics/Regression/LeastSquares/{Defs, CriticalRadius, Localization, LocalGaussianComplexity, MasterErrorBound, Linear/*, L1/*}.lean

Boucheron · Lugosi · Massart

Concentration Inequalities

Concentration Inequalities: A Nonasymptotic Theory of Independence (Oxford University Press, 2013).

Chapter 3

Efron–Stein and variance bounds

Conditional expectations by coordinate, tensorized variance decompositions, and the Efron–Stein inequality.

Statlib/Probability/Concentration/EfronStein.lean · Statlib/Probability/Gaussian/Poincare/EfronStein.lean
Chapter 4

The entropy method

Entropy duality, decomposition, Han-type inequalities, and subadditivity under product measures.

Statlib/Probability/Entropy/{Basic, Duality, Variational}.lean · Statlib/Probability/Entropy/Conditional/{Basic, Decomposition, Subadditivity}.lean
Chapter 5

Log-Sobolev inequalities and Herbst

The two-point inequality, Bernoulli tensorization, passage to Gauss space, Gaussian log-Sobolev, Poincaré, and Lipschitz concentration.

Statlib/Probability/Concentration/LogSobolev/* · Statlib/Probability/Gaussian/Poincare/* · Statlib/Probability/Gaussian/LipschitzConcentration.lean

Mohri · Rostamizadeh · Talwalkar

Foundations of Machine Learning

Foundations of Machine Learning, 2nd ed. (MIT Press, 2018).

Rademacher methods

Complexity, uniform convergence, and linear predictors

Empirical and expected Rademacher complexity, finite sign-vector calculus, symmetrization, expected and high-probability uniform-deviation bounds, McDiarmid’s inequality, Massart’s finite-class lemma, empirical Dudley chaining, and complexity bounds for ℓ¹- and ℓ²-bounded linear predictors. The completed merge integrates the lean-rademacher development into the library’s common probability and empirical-process infrastructure.

Statlib/LearningTheory/Rademacher/{Defs, Signs, Symmetrization, Complexity, Massart, Dudley}.lean · Statlib/LearningTheory/UniformDeviation/{Defs, BoundedDifference, Bounds}.lean · Statlib/LearningTheory/FunctionClass/LinearPredictor/{L1, L2}.lean

Selected Theorems

Uniform deviation · Statlib.LearningTheory

Uniform law via Rademacher complexity

For a separable, first-countable parameter space indexing measurable functions bounded in absolute value by \(b>0\), with pointwise-continuous parametrization, empirical means track their expectations uniformly.

\[ \mathbb{P}_{S\sim\mu^{\otimes n}}\!\left( \sup_{f\in\mathcal{F}}\left|P_n f-Pf\right| \ge 2\mathfrak{R}_n(\mathcal{F})+\varepsilon \right) \le \exp\!\left(-\frac{n\varepsilon^2}{2b^2}\right). \]
Statlib/LearningTheory/UniformDeviation/Bounds.lean · theorem uniform_deviation_tail_bound_separable_of_pos
Least squares · LeastSquares.linear_minimax_rate

Finite-sample guarantee for linear regression

For a \(d\)-dimensional linear class and injective design, least squares achieves the optimal finite-sample rate.

\[ \exists\,C_1,C_2>0:\qquad \mathbb{P}_{w\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I_n)}\!\left( \left\lVert \widehat{f}(w)-f^\star\right\rVert_n^2 \le C_1\frac{\sigma^2 d}{n} \right) \ge 1-\exp(-C_2d). \]
Statlib/Statistics/Regression/LeastSquares/Linear/MinimaxRate.lean · theorem LeastSquares.linear_minimax_rate
Gaussian functional inequality

Gaussian logarithmic Sobolev inequality

\[ \operatorname{Ent}_{\gamma_n}(g^2) \le 2\int_{\mathbb{R}^n} \left\lVert \nabla g(x)\right\rVert_2^2 \,\mathrm{d}\gamma_n(x). \]
Statlib/Probability/Concentration/LogSobolev/GaussianTensorization.lean · theorem GaussianLSI.gaussian_logSobolev_W12_pi
Dudley’s entropy integral · Statlib.Probability.Process.Dudley

Expected supremum of a sub-Gaussian process

For an anchored sub-Gaussian process indexed by a totally bounded set of diameter at most \(D\), the expected supremum is controlled by metric entropy.

\[ \mathbb{E}_{\mu}\!\left[\sup_{t\in s} X_t\right] \le 12\sqrt{2}\,\sigma \int_{0}^{D}\sqrt{\log \mathcal{N}(s,d,\varepsilon)}\,\mathrm{d}\varepsilon . \]
Statlib/Probability/Process/Dudley.lean · theorem dudley

Installation

1. Add the dependency

Add Statlib to your project’s lakefile.toml.

[[require]]
name = "Statlib"
git  = "https://github.com/Lean-MoDS/Statlib.git"

2. Import a chapter

Import only the part of the library you need.

import Statlib.Probability.Process.Dudley
import Statlib.LearningTheory.UniformDeviation.Bounds
import Statlib.Statistics.Regression.LeastSquares.Linear.MinimaxRate

Statlib · Non-asymptotic statistics verified in Lean 4.

Apache 2.0
